qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: WebSocket message length integer overflow enables memory exhaustion denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a WebSocket frame parser integer overflow vulnerability in lib/facil/http/parsers/websocket_parser.h, while the advisory describes a completely different vulnerability: a JSON parser infinite loop bug in lib/facil/fiobj/fio_json_parser.h triggered by input starting with 'i' or 'I'. These are different parsers (WebSocket vs JSON), different code locations, different root causes (integer overflow vs infinite loop), and different CWEs (CWE-190/CWE-400 vs CWE-400/CWE-835). The finding does not identify the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes CVE-2026-41146: a JSON parser infinite loop vulnerability in fio_json_parse when encountering nested JSON values starting with 'i' or 'I', affecting facil.io and the iodine Ruby gem. The only qualifying finding reports a completely different vulnerability: a WebSocket frame parser integer overflow in websocket_parser.h that accepts exabyte-scale payload lengths without proper validation. These vulnerabilities have different root causes (infinite loop in numeral handling vs integer overflow in length validation), different parsers (JSON parser in lib/facil/fiobj/fio_json_parser.h vs WebSocket parser in lib/facil/http/parsers/websocket_parser.h), different attack vectors (malformed JSON vs malformed WebSocket frame), and different security consequences (CPU spin vs memory exhaustion). No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.
evidence: Advisory CVE-2026-41146: JSON parser infinite loop in lib/facil/fiobj/fio_json_parser.h lines 434-468, triggered by input '[i' or '[""i' that enters infinite loop in numeral handling
Finding: WebSocket parser integer overflow in lib/facil/http/parsers/websocket_parser.h lines 407-409, accepting payload lengths up to 2^62-1 bytes without memory validation
Advisory affects both facil.io and iodine gem via vendored parser; finding only affects facil.io WebSocket functionality
Advisory CWEs: CWE-400 (uncontrolled resource consumption), CWE-835 (loop with unreachable exit condition); Finding CWEs: CWE-190 (integer overflow), CWE-400
Different vulnerable code paths: JSON parser at fio_json_parser.h vs WebSocket parser at websocket_parser.h
